name: DIPTYCH - raw vs refined, scroll-synced
brief: Vertical split screen: left half shows the raw/local output, right half the hosted/refined version of the same subject, scroll-synced. Center seam is a draggable or scroll-driven wipe revealing the quality jump. The comparison IS the story.

name: SPOTLIGHT - noir, cursor light reveals work from darkness
brief: Pitch-black stage. A soft spotlight follows the cursor; works are only visible where the light falls, emerging from darkness as you sweep. On click the light locks and blooms to reveal the full piece + credit. Dramatic, tactile, mysterious.

name: FILM STRIP - scrub a 35mm strip
brief: A literal horizontal 35mm film strip (sprocket holes, frame borders, edge codes) that you scrub left/right. Each frame is an asset; the centered frame enlarges/plays. Frame counter + timecode in mono. Reads like a cutting-room reel.

name: LIQUID - WebGL displacement on hover/transition
palette: near-black + one accent, media brings color
brief: WebGL plane with a displacement/fluid shader: works ripple and melt on hover and morph-dissolve between each other on transition. A liquid cursor trail. Cap DPR<=2; mobile falls back to clean CSS crossfades. The distortion is the signature.

name: CANVAS - pan/zoom infinite board of works
palette: near-black grid or bone, one accent
brief: An infinite zoomable/pannable 2D canvas (drag to pan, scroll to zoom) where all works are scattered spatially like a mood board. A minimap + zoom-to-fit. Feels like exploring a designer's workspace, not a page.

name: SWISS - Bauhaus/International poster grid
palette: bone #f0ece1 + ink + one primary (red or blue)
brief: Each work is a strict Swiss/International-style poster: hard modular grid, Helvetica-scale type, big numerals, primary color blocks, generous negative space, baseline rhythm. Disciplined, timeless, print-grade. Light mode.

name: DASHBOARD - present the reel as a live ops console
brief: Frame the work as a real-time generation ops dashboard: status panels, live tickers, sparkline metrics, a 'jobs' table where each row is an asset (model, latency, cost, status) that expands to preview the output. Data-dense, precise, beautiful dev-tool aesthetic.

name: COVERFLOW - 3D reflective carousel
brief: Works as cards on a 3D cover-flow carousel with a reflective floor; arrow/drag to flip through, the centered card faces you and plays. Smooth easing, depth-of-field on the sides. Cap DPR<=2; mobile = swipe stack fallback.

name: ZINE - DIY cut-paper collage, anti-design
brief: Raw DIY zine: torn-paper edges, tape, photocopied halftone textures, handwritten scrawl, rotated cut-outs, overlapping. Deliberately anti-polished and energetic. Works pasted in like a scrapbook. Scroll feels like flipping a homemade zine.

name: DEPTH - multi-layer parallax diorama
brief: Each scene is a multi-layer parallax diorama: foreground, midground, work, and background move at different rates on scroll/mouse, giving real depth. Works float in a dimensional space. Pure CSS/transform (cheap, smooth).

name: BOOT - OS/terminal boot loads each work
brief: A faux boot/OS sequence: a terminal prints init lines and a progress bar 'loads' each asset, then the work fills the screen with a CRT power-on flash. Between works, a quick 'loading next module' beat. Nostalgic-tech, rhythmic.

name: COVER - each section is a magazine cover
palette: bold, varies per cover but disciplined
brief: Every full-screen section is composed as a magazine cover for that work: masthead, issue/date, cover lines, barcode, the work as cover image. Scroll = flipping through an issue archive. Strong editorial hierarchy.

name: RING - works on a rotating 3D cylinder
brief: Works mapped onto the inside/outside of a slowly rotating 3D cylinder/ring you spin (drag or scroll). The front-facing work is sharp + plays; others curve away. Hypnotic, gallery-in-the-round. Cap DPR<=2; mobile = horizontal snap fallback.

name: BLUEPRINT - SVG line-art draws to frame each work
brief: Animated SVG line-art (a blueprint/schematic) draws itself on scroll, its strokes framing and pointing to each work as it resolves. Technical, precise, like an engineering schematic that builds the reel. Stroke-dashoffset animations.

name: ESSAY - typographic long-read with works as figures
brief: A reading-first typographic essay/manifesto about generating media, with the works embedded inline as captioned figures (fig. 01...) that animate in as you reach them. Strong reading column, pull-quotes, footnotes. Calm, intelligent, content-led.

name: GLITCH - datamosh/RGB-split transitions
brief: Works arrive and depart via datamosh/glitch: RGB channel split, scanlines, block displacement, pixel-sort sweeps on transition; a subtle persistent VHS shimmer. Edgy, digital-decay aesthetic. Keep transitions short; reduced-motion = clean cuts.
